name: simhash
description: SimHash 64-bit content fingerprint for near-duplicate / SEO-canonical detection. Tokenizes a document into [a-z0-9] runs, FNV-1a-hashes each token as a native u64, folds token hashes into 64 per-bit counters, and emits the sign-of-counter fingerprint. The win over JS is the native i64: a faithful JS baseline must use BigInt because f64 cannot hold exact 64-bit integers.

alloc.doc: reserve byteLen bytes (8-byte aligned), growing linear memory; returns byte ptr

simhash.doc: fingerprint the docLen bytes at docPtr; accPtr is 256B scratch (zeroed internally); writes u32[2] {low, high} of the 64-bit fingerprint to outPtr

memory.name: doc
memory.note: document bytes, one per char, lowercased ASCII by the host; codes >255 folded to space

memory.name: acc
memory.note: per-bit signed counters; zeroed at the start of each simhash() call

notes: Tokenization: a token is a maximal run of bytes in [a-z0-9] (codes 97-122 or 48-57); every other byte is a separator. FNV-1a: h=0xcbf29ce484222325, per byte h^=c then h*=0x100000001b3 mod 2^64. Fingerprint bit b = 1 iff counter[b] > 0. Near-duplicates are documents whose fingerprints differ in few bits (small Hamming distance); the engine's hamming(a,b) helper computes that distance. No imports, no SIMD.
